title: succisa pratensis subsp. hirsuta
intro: Succisa pratensis subsp. hirsuta, commonly known as hairy devil's-bit or rough devil's-bit, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. Consuming the plant or using its herbal preparations can provide various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Succisa pratensis subsp. hirsuta can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
